title: How We Became an AI-First Company in 45 Days
transcript: I'm shooting this video on 24th March 2026 and by this time several changes have happened in the world of AI. In fact, our own team has seen a humongous transformation. Our team has vibe coded over 35 applications, pieces of software, tools, automation workflows, and so on. We have replaced several SaaS products that we were using and we cancelled a few subscriptions already. Our operation head has built an interview prep tool. Our finance manager has built a website for our foundation. Our analytics head built an internal BI tool. Our analyst built an AI video reviewer. Our AI head built my Hindi voice clone. Our marketing head built a SaaS tool that replaces the need of the graphics designer. Hem built an internal assessment tool that would have otherwise costed $20,000. And I myself built a mobile app for CodeBasics, which is being beta tested right now. On the whole, 12 plus tools are in production right now. All of this happened in 45 days. In a regular timeline without AI, this would have taken at least 1 year. Yes, 365 days, but [music] we did this in 45 days. Let's do the math. This equation means 1 day in this new AI era is equal to 8 days in the old ways of working. Folks, let's get into this time [music] capsule. I'm going to take you into this 45 days real journey. In a way, it began with an email. >> [snorts] [music] >> So, I read the mail on a Thursday morning as soon as I got up. It The subject itself was intriguing. But you know how risky it can get. Once it become live, that will give them kick. That will make them happy. If they are happy, obviously my colleagues are happy, then obviously I'm happy. So, I was having this chat with our CTO Ashok and Siddhant and Mahavir. I was telling them that their only job is to make sure that products and tools, they go to production. And their only job was to get it deployed. And it is not easy because there are a lot of deployment requests coming left, right and center and most of the times the tools are not tested. So, what they did, they came up with these cloud skills which is really good. You could say create a set of skills and deploy it in the GitHub which will make all the testing easier for the people. So, they can self test. They can do all the integration testing, penetration testing, security testing before it comes to them. Chandraish took care of orchestration among the people and among the tools. I would say it's a very important skill to have in this AI era because a lot of people are not technical. If you have to collaborate with them, communicate with them and get them, you know, set up with GitHub, you know, hosting on servers, it requires a certain kind of a skill. So, I'm thinking from very different angle. Now, I'm thinking like a security engineer. I'm thinking like a >> [snorts] >> tester. I'm thinking like DevOps guy. DevOps guy. We could finally see things on prod not just working, but also secure and that was a great motivator for everyone. While things were mostly great, we did face the reality which are those challenges [music] and I don't think any team can go through this transformation without facing that reality. The major wipe coding challenge with non-technical people is of course they're not technical. They're going to take some time to set up the basics even even with GitHub. That was okay because we had our technical team who was really committed to help these people. I have seen my technical people sitting with non-technical folks and helping them understand the basics. That was really how we overcame this challenge. The other challenge we had is with integrating the new wipe coded tools with our existing system. We created a lot of wipe coded tools and it was easy for us to deploy the tools which is not connected to our internal systems because with our internal systems it requires an additional layer of security, additional layer of scrutiny and it took some extra time. We are figuring out ways to solve that. The biggest challenge of all is to set the people with the right mindset. There is this amazing creative person in my team. She came to me and said that AI is killing her creativity and she's not able to feel creative when she's working with AI. I think she totally misunderstood the concept. Then I sat with them and spoke to her and finally she came up with amazing output. She created this comic video using AI and it did not kill her creativity. In fact, boosted her creativity. It helped her to create more stuff. Then we also had our developers saying that it it is not easy for them to switch from hand coding to this wipe coding because they are used to do that. That's their daily bread. They used to open this terminal and type on hand and suddenly they are writing English and asking the AI to code. It is not easy for them. Like it's absolutely difficult to digest as well for us because because at the end of the day it it looks like AI >> [laughter] >> And they also felt that what is the relevance of them in the team. And that's another mental struggle my team went through, but we all sat together, spoke about it very openly and today they are still very relevant in the team because they are the ones who are setting up the guardrails to deploy stuff. So, yes, that's another challenge you would have to face if you're doing this thing in your team. As you see, it's not always a rosy picture. There are certain things, protocol that you need to follow in order to make wipe coded applications work in production and to give our learning journey in form of a clear takeaway so that you can use this learning lessons for your transformation. >> [music] >> Number one is you need to be with like-minded people. In my team, people have this attitude that we will get it done. They don't doubt themselves for being non-technical. They have the mindset to hack the internet and [music] find a solution. Number two is you need people with proper technical expertise. In our team, we had three to four people who can set guardrails and guide us, who can help with production deployment, [music] scaling and so on. You can't leave everything to AI. Number three is you need an orchestrating personality. You need to orchestrate among the people, [music] among the tools and this is a legit skill that you need in this AI era. Number four is you need to know how to distribute. It starts with distributing your own talent which means personal branding. In our team, we have asked our team members to build personal brand from day one and many of them are active on LinkedIn and Instagram. >> [music] >> Multitasking with AI agent. Number five, be ready to unlearn so that you can learn a new thing. Stop doing things in old ways and if you [music] are an experienced professional, this is going to be very difficult thing, but this is what is needed in this new [music] age.
